title: Level 729
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 729: "Pastel Fields"}}{{Headerbox|file=Longerscrolling.jpg|header='Pastel colors dull reality.'|speed=25s}} {{Background|file=Background field.png}} {{Class 2e}} '''Level 729''' is the 730th level of the backrooms ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Pastel hills.jpg|A pastel pink field with cyan grass.|width=410x410px|caption=A photo of '''Level 729''', photograph provided by The Shepherds.}} Level 729 is a hilly field of light, vibrant grain, roughly a foot high. Cutting through the landscape are small streams of a viscous, opaque liquid. This liquid has been tested and is shown to be a dilute form of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]]. Collection of the liquid is impossible, as this variant evaporates extremely quickly in any container outside of the river it originates from. This property is distinct from any artificial reproduction of the liquid. The topography of the level is unusual compared to many other outdoor levels in the Backrooms. The locations of objects and structures remains constant and unchanging. However, the elevation of the terrain appears to change extremely quickly while unobserved. This creates rivers that flow on the sides of hills, rather than between them. While the rivers within level 729 are constantly flowing, there seems to be no one direction they flow to. Due to their blatant ignorance of gravity, they flow in an erratic pattern and collide with one another on intersections constantly. Another strange property of these rivers is that anything, including people, that is completely submerged under the surface of the water is transported to another part of the level when it exits the liquid. The distance teleported from the origin point appears to be limited to the 5 kilometer radius of maximum stability. It is not recommended to do this, as prolonged exposure to the river can cause skin irritation and hives. The grain that is always present in Level 729 appears to be very similar to [[The Frontrooms]]'s species of Common Wheat, although with several differences. The grain's color is the most noticeable difference, being a pastel pink color. Other differences include a fuzz that grows on the seeds, impossibly dense roots, and a resistance to rotting. A compacted form of this grain composes the floor of the main area in Level 729. This grain can easily be milled, mixed with Almond Water, and baked into a sweet bread. This bread contains an alarming amount of sugar, and is allegedly "Very Filling" from those who have consumed it. Analysis of this bread has shown to contain absolutely zero nutritional value, with no vitamins, minerals, or anything that could possibly feed a human being. When dehydrating the dough, it leaves nothing behind but sugar. Venturing out past approximately 5 kilometers, the ground is softer and behaves like a thick clay. Going approximately 50 kilometers, the ground is similar to mud, with clumps of compressed grain scattered inside of it. After passing approximately 125 kilometers, the grain loses detail, with each stem resembling ribbons or thick paper painted to look like grain. Additionally, the muddy ground becomes much more difficult to wade through. Any further than 250 kilometers the level ceases to have collision. It is impossible to go past this point in the level, nor is there any reason to do so. ===Communication=== Communicating to the outside of this level is possible, but receiving transmissions from within is impossible. All audio and video messages to anybody inside of Level 729 will not be received. All text messages cannot be sent. Communication between two people within Level 729 is unaffected. Radios will constantly play a strange song with no lyrics while turned on, on any frequency. ===Known Structures=== Scattered throughout Level 729 are various greenhouses. They are not very common, only 14 being discovered so far. The glass in these greenhouses appears to be completely indestructible. Along with these, the greenhouses contain very little objects, at most a table or an empty crop holder. The Shepherds have claimed most of them for residence and trade. ===Dangers=== The bread produced from level 729's grain is the only food source that naturally exists in this level. Due to the nonexistant nutritional composition of this bread, malnutrition is inevitable without leaving Level 729, which is a challenge in its own right. Along with this, the grain acts similar to an opioid inside the body shortly after consumption. This can result in an addiction, but The Shepherds offer recovery. Allegedly, it is not difficult to lose the addiction. ==Entities== There is only one type of entity that exists here. ===Polarites=== At darker times of the cycle, Polarites emerge from the ground. Polarites are humanoid figures with bicolor patterns covering their bodies, although they are very hard to see. Polarites walk through the fields of Level 729 aimlessly, and do not show any other behavior. If any object that isn't from Level 729 comes in contact with a Polarite, the Polarite will immediately vanish from visibility. Polarites are impossible to photograph, touch, hear, or smell. ==Colonies and Outposts== Numerous greenhouses are scattered around Level 729, their locations are definite and unchanging. These act as checkpoints, stops, and common places to meet. Most are in occupation by The Shepherds. ===The Shepherds - Continuous=== *About 12 members, constantly changing *Settled in a greenhouse *Willing to and insistent on trading *Many members were scarred by other levels *Grow their own food and recycle water *Rarely leave the level to get nutritious rations *New members are welcome
'''pak.int.warning.txt'''
{{Background|file=TheBack.png}}
{{Blurquote|image=BadThing.png|quote-text=This place is, shockingly, not safe. I don't know when this message will get out, but hopefully MEG realizes that the Shepherds are lying to them about how awful it is. I've seen corpses mummified in beds, their mouths stained pink from chewing stalks, too weak to knead bread by themselves and too hungry to communicate. If you're stuck here, don't trust the Shepherds. Their addiction recovery program is an axe. You'll make it through by yourself. Eventually.|person=name}}
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Tripping in a patch of pink grain in [[Level 10]] will lead here. *If a wanderer mixes a combination of [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] and [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and dips their hand in it, they will pass out and wake up in Level 729. ===Exits=== *If a wanderer consumes a single seed of the grain, it will temporarily lead them to any prime numbered level (EX: 1, 29, 13). Shortly following said wanderer sleeping, they are anomalously transported to Level 729 *Traveling 250 kilometers out from the starting area will lead a wanderer to levels in-between [[Level 9]] and [[Level 17]].
'''Authors and Contributors'''
FellowTraveler - Main writer, minor CSS. Reject human return to monkey - CSS and Wikitext. PlutoniumOnInternet - Categorization Zeyang Chen - Helpful color correction
